title: equity-lock --flatten-mode all CANNOT close a Stage-2 crypto position > volume_max (close_position never chunks -> retcode 10014, never self-heals)
source: LIVE INCIDENT 2026-06-22 night: CEO ordered a crypto flatten via --flatten-at/--flatten-mode all. ETH (291.77) and SOL (1163.1) > volume_max(100) -> equity_lock close_position rejected every tick with retcode=10014 (invalid volume); BTC + FX/metals closed fine. The big crypto legs stayed OPEN; operator had to flatten manually in chunks with the live service stopped (exclusive access) to avoid a race with the still-engaged equity-lock.
why: MT5Broker.close_position closes EACH ticket at its FULL volume (line ~769). On a NETTING account the Stage-2 bet is ONE ticket whose lots exceed volume_max(100), so the close order is rejected (10014) and NOTHING closes - on EVERY tick, forever (no self-heal). The runbook claims the equity-lock flatten 'self-heals across ticks', but that only applies to the REBALANCE path (_reduce_position, which clamps each close to volume_max and iterates); the equity-lock 'all' flatten path uses close_position, which does NOT chunk. Net effect: a deliberate cut/final --flatten-mode all CANNOT flatten a large Stage-2 crypto position - the single most safety-critical operator action silently fails on exactly the position it most needs to close.
value_rationale: Directly safety-critical: the cut/final flatten and any emergency stand-down depend on this working for the Stage-2 bet, which is by construction larger than volume_max. Manual chunked close under time pressure (what we did tonight) is error-prone (race with the autonomous equity-lock, stale position reads, near-miss short-flip).
scope: TDD: failing test - equity-lock 'all' flatten of a single netting position of N*volume_max lots must close it to zero across repeated ticks (or in one call via a chunking loop), with a fake broker that rejects any single order > volume_max (retcode 10014).

Fix: route the equity-lock flatten (and the DD/kill flatten) through a chunked close that clamps each order to volume_max and ITERATES until flat (reuse _reduce_position semantics), OR make close_position itself loop opposite/by-ticket closes <= volume_max until the position is flat.
Re-read broker net before each chunk; never oversell (no short-flip).
Surface a loud [ALARM] only if NO progress over K ticks (genuine stuck), not on the expected multi-tick grind.
Full suite green incl parity + tests/test_volume_max_clamp_alarm.py.
recommendation: Make the equity-lock/kill flatten use the chunked _reduce_position loop (same primitive the rebalance already uses) so a >volume_max position is driven to zero by repeated clamped closes. This is the smallest change that makes the most safety-critical action actually work.
out_of_scope: No change to sizing, the lambda policy, or strategy. Pure close-path robustness.

history.reason: TDD fix: chunked close_position for >volume_max netting positions
Chunked _close_ticket to flatten >volume_max positions; live-incident fix 2026-06-22; full suite green; code review SAFE-TO-MERGE; committed 2f351e4 on fix/b0211-chunked-flatten
